headline:novelty

perItemFindings.label: headline
perItemFindings.one_line: Delivered tier subfield < floor field; directed reroute: Specify a concrete hypothesis- and law-independent score-Jacobian learner and derive both its L4 rate and the tilted bound log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) from the stated Gaussian-design and envelope primitives; then prove that the resulting score-contrast rule attains the Chernoff exponent throughout the supercritical regime.

review.verbatim_critique:
[D0.5.G cold referee] delivered tier=subfield below novelty_target=field (floor=field). The delivered novelty is confined to an engineered two-node exchange-symmetric path, after which the minimax frontier and elbow largely follow standard simple-versus-simple Chernoff and local-normal testing arguments. The score-contrast result proves only sign consistency conditional on the abstract jacobian-estimability assumption; it supplies neither a concrete trainer satisfying that assumption nor any attainable error exponent. Calling its L4 scale a "sharp supercritical sign-consistency condition" overclaims the theorem because no necessity or converse is proved. The TL;DR's opening statement about nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANMs is also broader than the proved result, which establishes identification only along the specified mirror paths. Closing primitive tilted attainability for an explicit learner would convert the likelihood benchmark into a usable score-based rate result and could lift the note to field level.

DIRECTED IMPROVEMENT (re-derive the SAME object implementing this, do not reproduce the prior note): Specify a concrete hypothesis- and law-independent score-Jacobian learner and derive both its L4 rate and the tilted bound log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) from the stated Gaussian-design and envelope primitives; then prove that the resulting score-contrast rule attains the Chernoff exponent throughout the supercritical regime.
